title: YBW leadership talk — 'Live to work or work to live' (Aug 11 2019)
html:
19-minute keynote captured by Yahir at Yahir Business Week / Oregon Entrepreneurs camp in corvallis or (Aug 11 2019, just before osu Fall term start). Speaker: an Aggie general manager with five resin-tech patents who runs a $110M region for Pentair Water Technologies. Sits beside the Portland VC sales talk from four days later — the two recordings together are Yahir's August 2019 leadership-archetype download.

TL;DR

  • Speaker frames career as deliberate skill-stacking: chemist → R&D → marketing → sales → board → GM, choosing each job by which skills the next one needs — same regret minimization framework / do things that dont scale logic Yahir later uses for his own austin move sequencing.
  • "Live to work or work to live" — a binary the speaker says you must choose now, not defer; pairs with Yahir's later fear of not doing and burn bright die young thinking.
  • Leadership = winning hearts (people), management = winning the how (logic + prioritization). Speaker's bacon-and-eggs framing: chicken is involved in breakfast, pig is committed — sets up max effort commit as the takeaway word for the YBW cohort.
  • "God gave you two ears and one mouth in that ratio for a reason" — listening as the trust-earning move; complements asking the right question and the leader as network not iq frame Yahir is building toward.
  • Speaker tells YBW to model the collective sense of purpose this week — challenge the status quo, accept ownership, don't accept weakness.

Key quote

"You have to choose today if you're gonna live to work, or if you're gonna work to live. Nobody's gonna make that choice for you."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Provenance for the career-as-skill-staircase mental model that shows up in Yahir's later career archetypes spy leader creator thinking and his Austin pivot.
  • One of the only formal leadership talks Yahir has on tape from the grants pass teen era → osu handoff window — a load-bearing influence node.
  • Pair with 2019 08 15 voice memo 1852 — same week, complementary lens (sales-first vs. skill-stack-first).

Open questions

  • Confirm speaker identity (referenced "Pentair Water Technologies", $1.5B "startup" with 100 M&A; very likely a Pentair executive). Not stubbing until 2nd mention surfaces.

yebw · corvallis or · osu · max effort commit · regret minimization framework · career archetypes spy leader creator · asking the right question · leader as network not iq
